message:
feat(http/h1): client codec security limits + chunked response encoding

Client codec now has configurable security limits matching server codec:
- DEFAULT_MAX_HEADERS_SIZE (64 KiB), DEFAULT_MAX_BODY_SIZE (16 MiB)
- max_headers_size() and max_body_size() builder methods
- Overflow checks during header parsing before headers_end
- Body size limit check for Content-Length before state transition
- RFC 7230 3.3.3 compliance: reject responses with both headers

Chunked body decoding security hardening (client-side):
- decode_chunked() now takes max_body_size parameter
- Size limit checked before accumulating each chunk
- CRLF validation after chunk data and terminal chunk

Response encoder now supports chunked Transfer-Encoding:
- Detects Transfer-Encoding: chunked header
- Skips Content-Length header when chunked (per RFC 7230)
- Encodes body as: {size}\r\n{data}\r\n0\r\n\r\n
- Empty body encoded as just terminal chunk: 0\r\n\r\n
- New test: encode_chunked_response validates output format

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
